ControllerEndpoint.description: How the platform reaches a greenhouse's controller — registry metadata supplied at registration (platform data model §1). The SPA never speaks these directly; only the platform does.

rest_base_url.description: Base URL of the controller's REST API on the local Docker network (e.g. http://gh-a:8080).

mqtt_topic_root.description: Root of the controller's MQTT topic tree (e.g. gh/gh-a).

GreenhouseSummary.description: One greenhouse's identity and live rollup for the fleet view (frontend purpose-and-views §1). A compact current-vs-target readout backs the fleet card; the full target bundle is on the detail endpoint.

crop.description: Assigned crop label, or null when none is assigned (a label only — the controller is crop-agnostic).

drift.description: (2b) True when the controller's reported setpoints differ from the platform's intended state (reconciliation, platform §5).

time_scale.description: Simulation-only: the controller's current simulated-clock speed multiplier (1.0 = real-time), backing the fleet card's per-greenhouse speed indicator. Null on a real-hardware controller or when unknown. Patched live by the WebSocket status frame (frontend-ws). Each controller's clock is independent (controller HAL §7).

climate.description: Compact current-vs-target readout for the fleet card.

temperature.description: Latest air-temperature reading (°C), or null when unavailable.

humidity.description: Latest relative-humidity reading (%RH), or null when unavailable.

co2.description: Latest CO₂ reading (ppm), or null when unavailable.

par.description: Latest PAR reading (µmol·m⁻²·s⁻¹), or null when unavailable.

setpoint_temperature.description: Currently-resolved temperature setpoint (°C), or null when unavailable.

GreenhouseDetail.description: A single greenhouse's snapshot for the detail view: its summary fields plus the full current target bundle (frontend data model §6, key ["greenhouse", id]).

GreenhouseRegistration.description: Operator registration of a greenhouse into the fleet (platform §3 — registration is operator tooling, served in 2a). The id is the operator-chosen slug reused everywhere.
